title: January 2026 City Walk Outcomes
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: March 5, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director
Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
DEPT: Community Engagement & Services
SUBJECT: January 2026 City Walk Outcomes
The City Walks Program is designed to enhance quality of life for residents while expediting service delivery to vulnerable populations.
Coordinated by Clean and Safe, City Walks are data-driven and strategically aligned with broader organizational initiatives. Using an interdepartmental approach, City Walks promote proactive problem-solving, timely interventions to address emerging Reno Direct service request trends, partners with community members to address concerns, and coordinates with the Reno Police Department's DEEDS (Directed Engagement and Enforcement Deployment Strategies) zones as a mechanism to connect with residents, businesses, services, and enforcement efforts.
City Walks take place four days per week, Monday to Thursday, including two days dedicated to City Walks within the Business Improvement District (BID) in partnership with the Downtown Reno Partnership.
City Walks Departments
• Community Engagement and Services – Clean and Safe
• Licensing and Code Enforcement
• Parks and Recreation – Park and River Rangers, based on location
• Reno Police – Community Action & Outreach (CAO), Mobile Engagement Team (MET), and Parking Enforcement
The following is an overview of City Walk operations and outputs for January 2026:

• 13 City Walks
• 36 Contacts with Community Members
• 15 Yards of Trash Collected
• 8 Occupied Vehicles Posted
• 1 Occupied Vehicle Towed
• 3 Citations
• 3 City Walks in DEEDS zone
Monday, January 5
• Locations: Oddie Blvd, Silverada Blvd, and Paradise Drive
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 1 individual was contacted and declined services
o 8 yards of trash were collected
Tuesday, January 6, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Along Ralston Street from 1st Street to 6th Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 1 individual was contacted and declined services
o 2 nuisance vehicles were issued 24-hour notice postings
Wednesday, January 7, DEEDS zone
• Locations: Gentry Way, Wrondel Way, Gallaway Lane, and Yori Avenue
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 1 nuisance vehicle was towed
Thursday, January 8, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Along 6th Street, from Ralston Street to Lake Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 1 service request submitted for graffiti abatement
January 2026 Highlights

• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services

o 2 individuals were contacted and declined services
Wednesday, January 14, DEEDS zone
• Locations: Oddie Blvd, Silverada Blvd, and Paradise Drive
• Departments in Attendance:

o 1 individual was contacted and declined services
o 4 yards of trash were collected
Thursday, January 15, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Along Ralston Street, from 1st Street to 6th Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
o Parking Enforcement
• Outcomes:
o 2 individuals were contacted and declined services
o 3 nuisance vehicles were issued 24-hour notice postings
o 2 abandoned vehicles were issued 72-hour notice postings
Tuesday, January 20, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Along Sierra Street, from 7th Street to 1st Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 2 individuals were contacted and declined services

Thursday, January 22, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Along Arlington Street, from 1st Street to Elm Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
o Park and River Rangers, Parks and Recreation

Monday, January 26, DEEDS zone
• Locations: Oddie Blvd, Silverada Blvd, and Fantastic Drive

Tuesday, January 27, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Along the Truckee River behind Greater Nevada Field, Wells underpass, and Brodhead Park
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
o Park and River Rangers, Parks and Recreation
• Outcomes:

o 1 citation was issued for sitting, lying, sleeping, or camping on a public place
Wednesday, January 28
• Locations: 8th Street Park, 7th Street, and Valley Road

Thursday, January 29, with the Downtown Reno Partnership

fields.authors: Cynthia Esparza, Director; Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
fields.subcategory: Clean and Safe / City Walks Program
fields.tldr: This memo reports on City Walk operations conducted throughout January 2026, covering 13 walks across multiple Reno neighborhoods and the downtown Business Improvement District. The interdepartmental program made 36 contacts with community members, collected 15 yards of trash, posted 8 occupied vehicles, towed 1 vehicle, and issued 3 citations. City Walks are coordinated by Clean and Safe and align with the Reno Police Department's DEEDS zones to connect residents and businesses with services and enforcement. Three of the 13 walks took place within designated DEEDS zones.
fields.key_stats: 13 City Walks conducted; 36 contacts with community members; 15 yards of trash collected; 8 occupied vehicles posted; 1 occupied vehicle towed; 3 citations issued; 3 City Walks in DEEDS zones; walks conducted 4 days per week (Monday–Thursday); 2 days per week in partnership with the Downtown Reno Partnership within the Business Improvement District
fields.action_items: 1 service request submitted for graffiti abatement (January 8); ongoing coordination with Downtown Reno Partnership for BID walks twice weekly; continued alignment of City Walks with DEEDS zone enforcement strategy
